items.title: Pahang Rangers team manager, family hurt in head-on collision in Terengganu
items.summary: KUANTAN: Pahang Rangers futsal team manager Muhammad Syahir Ab Aziz and his family were injured in a head-on collision at Setiu, Terengganu, on July 11. "Syahir and his family had attended the wedding of Pahang Rangers player Harith Naim (Mohd Nasir) and were on their way back to Kuantan. Pahang Rangers is a professional futsal club based in Kuantan that competes in the Malaysia Premier Futsal League.

items.title: Malaysia, South Korea launch AI academy to train digital talent
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: The Ministry of Digital today launched the Korea-Asean Digital Academy (KADA), a joint initiative with the Republic of Korea to develop industry-ready artificial intelligence (AI) and digital professionals in Malaysia. Coordinated by the Malaysia Digital Economy Corporation (MDEC), the programme will provide specialised training in AI and emerging digital technologies through four cohorts from 2026 to 2027, with a target of producing about 200 industry-ready Malaysian AI and digital professionals. "Through the programme, participants will undergo specialised training in AI and emerging digital technologies, equipping them with practical skills, global perspectives and industry competencies to meet the evolving demands of the digital economy.

items.title: Melaka police detain businessman over suspected involvement in 10 child sexual assault cases
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 13 — Police have detained a local businessman believed to be involved in 10 cases of sexual assault against boys between March and yesterday. Melaka police chief Datuk Dzulkhairi Mukhtar said the 36-year-old man was detained by members of the public at the Taman Desa Duyong community hall in Melaka at about 6pm yesterday before being handed over to police, according to national news agency Bernama. Police said the suspect allegedly approached the victims at various locations before carrying out acts that are being investigated under the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017.

items.title: Witness Protection (amendment) bill tabled for first reading
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Witness Protection (Amendment) Bill 2026, which aims to enhance witness protection and provide psychological support and post-protection assistance, was tabled for its first reading in the Dewan Rakyat today. According to a copy of the Bill uploaded to Parliament's official portal, one of the key amendments would allow a parent or guardian to apply for admission into the witness protection programme on behalf of a witness who lacks legal capacity. Clause 2 of the Bill seeks to amend subsection 7(3) of the Witness Protection Act 2009 (Act 696) by inserting the words "or lacks legal capacity" after the words "under the age of eighteen years."

items.title: Malaysia, South Korea team up to train 200 AI and digital professionals
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, July 13 — The Ministry of Digital today launched the Korea-Asean Digital Academy (KADA), a joint initiative with the Republic of Korea to develop industry-ready artificial intelligence (AI) and digital professionals in Malaysia. Coordinated by the Malaysia Digital Economy Corporation (MDEC), the programme will provide specialised training in AI and emerging digital technologies through four cohorts from 2026 to 2027, with a target of producing about 200 industry-ready Malaysian AI and digital professionals. “Through the programme, participants will undergo specialised training in AI and emerging digital technologies, equipping them with practical skills, global perspectives and industry competencies to meet the evolving demands of the digital economy.

items.title: Suspect nabbed after allegedly luring, molesting 10 young boys
items.summary: State police chief Datuk Zulkhairi Mukhtar said the suspect was apprehended by members of the public at 6pm yesterday at the Taman Desa Duyong community hall before being handed over to the police. He said initial investigations revealed the suspect was involved in 10 cases of physical sexual assault against 10 boys aged between eight and 13 from March to July this year. "Investigations showed the suspect met the victim again on July 12," Zulkhairi said.

items.title: Govt to study plastics industry proposals as global supply crisis hits sector, says Akmal
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Investment, Trade and Industry Ministry (MITI) and the Economy Ministry have been directed to examine industry proposals submitted on issues facing the industry in the midst of the global supply crisis, said Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir. "Industry feedback shows that manufacturers are facing pressure on raw material costs, logistics, cash flow, and delivery timeline uncertainty. "For some companies, the impact has already affected business contracts, investment decisions, and workforce management," he said in his briefing on the global supply crisis at Parliament Akmal said industry feedback showed manufacturers were facing pressure on raw material costs, logistics, cash flow, and delivery timeline uncertainty, with some companies already seeing impacts on business contracts, investment decisions, and workforce management.

items.title: Fertiliser Bill to be tabled in early 2027 to strengthen supply resilience, says Akmal
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government is expected to table a Fertiliser Bill in early 2027 to strengthen governance, quality, and market transparency in the sector, as it moves to shore up the resilience of agricultural input supply amid the global supply crisis, said Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir. "The Fertiliser Bill is expected to be tabled in early 2027 to strengthen governance, quality, and market transparency, while enhancing the resilience of agricultural input supply," he said in his briefing on the global supply crisis at Parliament, following a meeting of the National Economic Action Council (NEAC). Akmal said food security was the first matter discussed at today's NEAC meeting, alongside the pressure currently faced by the agricultural sector.

items.title: Alor Setar premise accused of being foreign-run found operated by Malaysians, says council
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, July 13 — The Alor Setar City Council (MBAS) today denied claims that a premise on Jalan Langgar was operated by foreigners, as alleged in a video that went viral on social media recently. Alor Setar Mayor Datuk Abdul Gafar Yahya said following the allegation, an inspection was carried out on July 9 by the MBAS Disease Control, Health Promotion and Environmental Health Inspectorate Division, Licensing Division and Enforcement Division, together with the Kota Setar District Health Office (PKD). “Checks also found that the premise has a valid business licence issued by MBAS,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Ringgit closes higher against major, regional currencies
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit ended higher against major and regional currencies, including the US dollar, on Monday amid continued geopolitical uncertainties in Middle East. At 6pm, the local currency edged up to 4.0680/0730 versus the greenback from 4.0695/0745 at last Friday's close. He said crude oil prices remained elevated, with West Texas Intermediate and Brent crude trading at US$73.02 and US$77.80 per barrel, respectively.

items.title: Geopolitics key to sukuk outlook, but 2026 issuance seen lower: Fitch
items.summary: Fitch expects global sukuk issuance in 2026 to remain below 2025 levels. He said credit fundamentals remain resilient, with no sukuk defaults since 2021 and more than 80 per cent of Fitch-rated sukuk carrying investment-grade ratings. Despite the slowdown in new issuance, global sukuk outstanding grew 11 per cent to US$1.1 trillion.

items.title: Wyndham, 1001 Nights showcase at Everse
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Wyndham Ion Majestic Hotel and 1001 Nights Hotel, part of NCT Group, will showcase their hospitality and tourism offerings at Everse 2026, a youth-focused event to be held at Hextar World, Empire City, from July 18-19. "At Everse, we will give visitors a glimpse of 1001 Nights Hotel and Wyndham Ion Majestic Hotel and why they are a must-visit destination in Genting Highlands. Located within NCT Group's flagship Grand Ion Majestic integrated development in Genting Highlands, 1001 Nights Hotel and Wyndham Ion Majestic Hotel sits approximately 6,000 feet above sea level, combining hospitality, leisure and lifestyle in a tranquil mountain setting.

items.title: Malaysia confident of 4-5pc growth in 2026 despite global headwinds, says economy minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 13 — The government remains bullish that Malaysia’s economy could grow between four to five per cent despite the International Monetary Fund (IMF) lowering its global economic growth projection to three per cent, Economy Minister Akmal Nasir Nasrullah said today. Akmal Nasir also highlighted Malaysia’s robust trade performance as the country’s total trade value jumped by 18.3 per cent to RM1.5 trillion between January and May this year. “Exports burgeoned by 24.3 per cent to RM793.8 billion while imports climbed by 11.8 per cent to RM661.1 billion, with a trade surplus of RM132.8 billion.

items.title: Migrant worker rights advocate accused of threatening Bangladesh journalists
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: British migrant worker rights advocate Andy Hall has been accused by a Bangladeshi news outlet of threatening journalists and making racist remarks following media coverage of his recent visit to Bangladesh, although the allegations have not been independently verified. The publication reported that Hall also held discussions with senior Bangladeshi officials on matters related to the Malaysia-Bangladesh labour migration corridor, as both countries work towards reopening Malaysia's labour market to Bangladeshi workers. Daily Tribunal 24 said Hall met Bangladesh's Expatriates' Welfare and Overseas Employment Minister, the prime minister's special assistant, recruitment agency representatives and non-governmental organisation officials.

items.title: TVET graduates to get job offers before graduation through industry links, says Zahid
items.summary: Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said the approach was implemented through early engagement between TVET institutions and companies as well as industry players to ensure the courses offered were aligned with labour market needs. Also present was Deputy Rural and Regional Development Minister Datuk Rubiah Wang. Ahmad Zahid said the government was also focusing on implementing High TVET courses to ensure graduates possessed skills that met industry demands.

items.title: NRD approves most MyKAS applications, over 1,000 citizenship cases still pending
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 13 — The National Registration Department (NRD) approved 286 MyKAS applications, or 96 per cent of the 298 applications received for the document between 2022 and May 31, 2026, Deputy Home Minister Datuk Seri Dr Shamsul Anuar Nasarah said. He said the department also received 3,117 late birth registration applications from the Indian community, of which 2,810 applications, or 90.1 per cent, had been approved, while another 251 were still being processed. “For citizenship applications, a total of 1,018 applications have been recorded.

items.title: New Admiralty Jurisdiction Bill aims to overhaul Malaysia’s maritime dispute framework
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 13 — The Admiralty Jurisdiction Bill 2026, which seeks to govern maritime matters and disputes within the shipping industry, has been referred to the Dewan Rakyat Special Select Committee for further scrutiny. Azalina said the Special Select Committee on the Admiralty Jurisdiction Bill 2026 would review the bill’s scope, structure and drafting before recommending any necessary amendments. “The Special Select Committee will prepare a statement setting out its views and recommendations on the bill, or propose a new bill, within three months.

items.title: PM’s power over prosecutor appointment stripped in revised AG separation Bill
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 13 — Putrajaya has proposed seven sweeping changes to a key reform Bill that it said would entirely isolate its top public prosecutor from executive and political influence, with Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said saying she hopes to have it tabled in Parliament’s Lower House this week. Azalina said in a statement the seven new safeguards underscores the Anwar government’s to reform. Azalina’s office said whether or not the revised Bill can be tabled this week itself will hinge on the Lower House approving the report.

items.title: Undercover officers, unmarked vehicles deployed in Pahang immigration raids
items.summary: KUANTAN: The Immigration Department has deployed undercover officers to track down undocumented foreigners after previous operations were compromised due to the suspected involvement of 'tonto'(lookouts). Pahang Immigration Department director Nursafariza Ihsan said that, as many undocumented foreigners had become familiar with the department's officers and official vehicles, new methods were needed to gather intelligence before launching special operations. Nursafariza said some employers continued to hire foreigners who abused their immigration passes by violating the conditions attached to them.

items.title: Psychologist says teen's diary entries were a temporary expression of anger
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: The late Zara Qairina Mahathir's angry expressions in her diaries were temporary and should not be taken as a reflection of her overall relationship with her mother, the coroner's court heard today. Child psychologist Dr Noor Aishah Rosli, 51, testified that when children or teenagers face problems, some choose to express their emotions by writing in diaries. "For this case, Zara was writing about her unhappiness only at that time," Noor Aishah said.

items.title: Malaysia has six months’ rice supply, local white rice price held at RM2.60/kg, says economy minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 13 — The country has adequate supply of food staples, including rice reserves that can last for six months, Economy Minister Akmal Nasir Nasrullah said today. Akmal Nasir said the government has also decided to maintain the price of local white rice — which currently stands at RM2.60 per kg — to avoid burdening Malaysians with higher food bills. “The average price of chicken dropped by 0.04 per cent to RM10.09 per kg while the price of ikan kembung dipped by 1.37 per cent to RM15.81 per kg.

items.title: Negri Sembilan polls: DAP faces battle against negative perception created by rivals
items.summary: SEREMBAN: DAP claims it will face a severe challenge following attempts by rival parties to play up racial narratives throughout the campaign period for the 16th Negri Sembilan election. Its secretary-general, Anthony Loke, said DAP knows its rivals would put the blame for issues in Negri Sembilan on it. "Everything is DAP's fault, and DAP must be rejected.

items.title: Volkswagen chief says 50,000 more job cuts may be needed to close competitive gap
items.summary: After already agreeing to 50,000 job cuts across the group, including its Porsche and Audi subsidiaries, the company must further reduce costs after calculating a 20 per cent cost disadvantage compared with comparable companies, Blume said in the memo seen by Reuters. This translates into a "theoretical deduction" of another 50,000 jobs worldwide, the memo said. "We are currently assessing across all brands, companies and regions how many adjustments are actually necessary and feasible," Blume said in the document.

items.title: Dewan Rakyat passes tougher rice laws to curb smuggling, market manipulation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 13 — The Dewan Rakyat today passed the Control of Padi and Rice (Amendment) Bill 2026 to strengthen enforcement, improve market regulation and protect the country’s paddy and rice supply chain. Deputy Agriculture and Food Security Minister Datuk Chan Foong Hin said the amendments were aimed at strengthening the government’s ability to regulate the paddy and rice industry, particularly in terms of enforcement, market control and industry development. Chan said the amendment would introduce stronger enforcement measures, including higher general penalties and specific penalties for certain offences, to deter irresponsible parties from manipulating the market.

items.title: DAP names Loke's aide Ho Weng Wah for Temiang seat
items.summary: SEREMBAN: DAP has named Transport Minister Anthony Loke's political secretary, Ho Weng Wah, as its candidate for the Temiang state seat in the 16th Negri Sembilan election, replacing incumbent Ng Chin Tsai. Loke, who is DAP secretary-general, said Ho is one of three new faces fielded by the party for the polls, alongside Lee Kai Yet in Mambau and S. Mugunthan in Seremban Jaya. Loke said the party had conducted a comprehensive assessment ahead of the election, including identifying potential new candidates as part of its efforts to rejuvenate the party.

items.title: Muhyiddin’s trial: Bumiputera company director says gets paid RM12,500 monthly, denies being used as alleged hidden shareholder’s nominee
items.summary: Aliza said the Companies Commission of Malaysia (SSM) records only showed Mohd Rizman Akum Khan’s and her name as the directors and shareholders of Nepturis, but added that they had a verbal understanding with Lian Tan Chuan (“TC Lian”) to split the shares among the three of them. Although Lian’s name is not in official records as a shareholder or director of Nepturis, Aliza insisted that he actually has a 30 per cent share in the company and its profits. Aliza: Disagree.

items.title: Plantation, commodity stocks lift Bursa Malaysia higher at close
items.summary: As for the active stocks, Tanco inched up 4.5 sen to 24 sen, Zetrix AI and VS Industry increased 1.5 sen each to 76.5 sen and 21 sen, respectively. HHRG put on half a sen to 11.5 sen while PUC was flat at 4.5 sen. Of the top gainers, United Plantations garnered 76 sen to RM35.02, Kuala Lumpur Kepong was up 28 sen to RM21.88, SD Guthrie added 21 sen to RM6.45, while Press Metal and Sarawak Oil Palms climbed 15 sen each to RM7.85 and RM5.15, respectively. Malaysian Pacific Industries led the losers list, falling RM1.70 to RM44.30, Fraser & Neave dipped 76 sen to RM28.94, Petronas Dagangan lost 34 sen to RM19.22, UWC slipped 29 sen to RM6.01, while Kelington erased 26 sen to RM7.70.

items.title: Congo's Ebola outbreak spreads to two more provinces
items.summary: DAKAR: The Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo has spread to two more northeastern provinces, Haut-Uele and Tshopo, the country's public health institute said in its latest report. The number of confirmed Ebola cases across the country rose to 1,926, including 702 deaths, official data showed late on Sunday. Four cases were recorded in Tshopo, including two deaths, with one death confirmed in Haut-Uele, as of Saturday.

items.title: Hormuz tensions: Malaysia assures adequate fuel supply until December, says economy minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 13 — Malaysia’s fuel supply remains stable and adequate despite fresh strikes from the United States (US) on Iran renewing concerns of shipping disruptions across the Strait of Hormuz, Economy Minister Akmal Nasir Nasrullah said today. Akmal Nasir said the government is working to ensure sufficient fuel supply until December by diversifying import sources, optimising local fuel production and strengthening long-term energy cooperation agreements. To further insulate Malaysia from energy shocks, Akmal Nasir said the government will continue negotiating a free trade deal between Malaysia and the European Union (EU), strengthen circular economy and consider suggestions to implement the Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) on a voluntary basis.

items.title: Malaysia can be global stingless bee honey market
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia has the potential to become a global hub for stingless bee honey as the industry moves towards international quality standards, scientific validation and wider commercial applications, according to Stingless Bee Factor Pte Ltd (SBF). To address these gaps, SBF launched the Future of Honey 2026 here, introducing what it described as the world's first international certification framework for stingless bee honey. The launch also highlighted scientific studies examining the nutritional and functional properties of stingless bee honey.

items.title: Six Myanmar nationals, including two children, held in immigration raid
items.summary: ALOR GAJAH: Two young boys were among six Myanmar nationals detained by the Melaka Immigration Department for operating a grocery shop selling imported goods and alcohol during a raid at Taman Mewah, Kelemak, today. At the same time, the two boys, aged one and seven, had no valid travel or identity documents. Anirwan Fauzee added that the operation targeted several premises in the Kelemak area believed to be employing or operated by foreign nationals.

items.title: Singapore president gets down to business with PM Anwar in 40-minute meeting in KL state visit
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 13 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim on Monday held a courtesy call with Singapore President Tharman Shanmugaratnam in conjunction with the latter’s three-day state visit to Malaysia. Total bilateral trade between the two countries reached RM402.35 billion (US$93.97 billion) in 2025, an increase of 1.5 per cent compared with 2024. From January to May this year, bilateral trade rose 14.5 per cent to RM189.90 billion (US$47.87 billion), compared with RM165.93 billion (US$37.65 billion) recorded during the corresponding period in 2025.

items.title: Two children among six Myanmar nationals held in Melaka grocery shop immigration raid
items.summary: ALOR GAJAH, July 13 — Two young boys were among six Myanmar nationals detained by the Melaka Immigration Department for allegedly operating a grocery shop selling various goods imported from Myanmar as well as alcoholic beverages during a raid at Taman Mewah, Kelemak, here today. Melaka Immigration director Anirwan Fauzee Mohd Aini said in the raid, conducted at about 10 am, four adults comprising three women and a man in their 40s were found to possess only passports, while the two boys, aged one and seven, had no valid travel or identity documents. In the same operation, Anirwan Fauzee said three Indian men working at a mamak restaurant in Taman Seri Kelemak were also detained for allegedly overstaying in the country.

items.title: Immigration seeks public help to find 43-year-old woman linked to Special Branch investigation, last seen in Semporna
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 13 — The Immigration Department is seeking public assistance to locate a local woman to facilitate an investigation by its Special Investigation Branch under the Enforcement Division at its headquarters. Members of the public with information on her whereabouts are urged to contact investigating officer Senior Deputy Assistant Director of Immigration Ella Fazlina Mat Misiah from the Special Investigation Branch (USK 5), Enforcement Division, Immigration Headquarters, at 03-8880 1211 during office hours. Alternatively, information may be channelled to the Immigration Operations Room at 03-8880 1555 after office hours.

items.title: Nancy Shukri tables Social Work Profession Bill, proposes council to oversee certification and ethics
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 13 —The Social Work Profession Bill 2026, which seeks to regulate social work practitioners, trainees and services, was tabled for first reading in the Dewan Rakyat today. According to the bill’s blue copy published on Parliament’s portal, the proposed legislation also seeks to establish the Malaysian Social Work Profession Council, which would be responsible for approving practice certification applications, developing professional competency standards, regulating the ethics and conduct of social work practitioners and trainees, and raising awareness of the profession. The bill also proposes establishing a Register of Social Work Practitioners and Social Work Trainees and outlines requirements for citizens applying for practice certification, temporary certification for non-citizens, and interim certification for those undergoing training and gaining experience in social work services.

items.title: Freedom of Information Bill to get closer look as govt sends it to select committee
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 13 — The government will table a motion tomorrow to refer the Freedom of Information Bill 2026 to a Parliamentary Select Committee (PSC), Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said announced today. She said the move would allow the Bill to undergo more thorough scrutiny through engagement with MPs and stakeholders. Azalina said the Bill is part of the Madani Government’s institutional reform agenda aimed at strengthening transparency, accountability and good governance, while giving the public lawful and structured access to official information.

items.title: Ukrainian drones hit 15 more Russian vessels in Sea of Azov
items.summary: KYIV: Ukrainian drones hit 15 Russian vessels in the Sea of Azov overnight, including seven tankers, Ukraine's drone forces commander said on Monday. In an earlier report, a Ukrainian drone struck a tanker as it was entering the Azov-Black Sea Canal, Yury Slyusar, governor of Russia's Rostov region, said on Sunday. The Ukrainian military has recently attacked more than 40 Russian tankers in the Sea of Azov as part of what Ukraine describes as a campaign aimed at disrupting fuel supplies to Russian forces and isolating Moscow-occupied Crimea.

items.title: Ipoh school starts tapping sun to cut electricity bill, Education Ministry plans rollout to other Perak schools
items.summary: IPOH, July 13 — Solar power systems installed at government-aided schools (SBK) could be expanded to more institutions to help reduce electricity costs, said Deputy Education Minister Wong Kah Woh. He said the pilot project under the Net Energy Metering (NEM) 3.0 scheme allowed schools to save around RM1,500 to RM1,700 in electricity bills each month through a Zero Capex approach, where solar panels are installed at no cost to schools. He said solar installations at schools could also serve as an educational platform, allowing students to understand renewable energy and environmental conservation through hands-on experience.

items.title: Influencer arrested for allegedly raping, taking lewd pictures of 14-year-old girl
items.summary: KANGAR: An influencer has been arrested for allegedly raping and taking lewd photographs of a 14-year-old girl at a rented house in Taman Kangar here yesterday. District police chief Assistant Commissioner Yusharifuddin Mohd Yusop said the 23-year-old suspect was arrested after the victim lodged a police report yesterday afternoon. The suspect took the victim for dinner at a restaurant in Bintong before bringing her to his rented house in Taman Kangar.

items.title: Dapsy Perak accuses Umno Youth of twisting Nga’s words, calls narrative misleading and dishonest
items.summary: IPOH, July 13 — DAP Socialist Youth (Dapsy) Perak today warned Ipoh Barat Umno Youth against twisting Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming’s statement for political mileage, saying his remarks had been deliberately taken out of context. The wing was responding to Ipoh Barat Umno Youth’s call for Nga, who is also the Kepayang assemblyman, to resign following Pakatan Harapan’s defeat in the Johor state election. https://www.facebook.com/sudin.kulop.31/posts/nga-kor-meng-sila-letak-jawatan-pemuda-umno-ipoh-barat-mendesak-yb-nga-kor-ming-/2118580669094225/ Dapsy Perak in a statement said the demand clearly showed Ipoh Barat Umno Youth had failed to understand the actual context of Nga’s earlier remarks.

items.title: Anwar pays courtesy call on Singaporean president during state visit
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today paid a courtesy call on Singaporean President Tharman Shanmugaratnam in conjunction with the latter's three-day state visit to Malaysia. The 40-minute meeting here was also attended by Foreign Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan, Investment, Trade and Industry Minister Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani and Entrepreneur and Cooperatives Development Minister Steven Sim, as well as senior officials from both countries. Singapore remains Malaysia's second-largest trading partner globally.

items.title: Hamas rejects top UN official accusation of Gaza aid obstruction
items.summary: TEL AVIV: Hamas rejected an accusation from a senior UN official on Monday of interfering with humanitarian deliveries in Gaza and intimidating aid workers. An official from the Hamas-run interior ministry in Gaza called the accusation unfounded. Hamas's actions "endangered humanitarian personnel, intimidated workers delivering life-saving food assistance and disrupted life-saving humanitarian operations", he said.

items.title: Defence minister says LTAT payouts higher than 5pc within reach, if its investments deliver
items.summary: KUANTAN, July 13 — The Armed Forces Fund Board (LTAT) is committed to maintaining competitive dividend payouts, subject to investment returns and the performance of its portfolio companies. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said LTAT had maintained a dividend rate of at least five per cent over the past two to three years, showing the agency’s resilience in navigating market challenges. “The dividends depend on LTAT’s investment returns.

items.title: MACC proposes 6 measures to tighten temple, church grant management
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has proposed six governance improvements to strengthen the management and disbursement of maintenance grants for non-Muslim houses of worship nationwide. The recommendations, submitted by the Negri Sembilan MACC to the Housing and Local Government Ministry, followed a governance review after investigations uncovered cases where recipients failed to carry out approved maintenance works despite receiving government funding. He also signed the Governance Improvement Report during the presentation ceremony, witnessed by Negri Sembilan MACC director Awgkok Ahmad Taufik Putra Awg Ismail.

items.title: Tune Talk expands focus from mobile services to digital ecosystems
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR : As Malaysia's telecommunications industry matures and mobile data becomes increasingly abundant, consumer expectations are evolving beyond traditional considerations such as data quotas and call plans. This was among the key topics discussed during a recent Beyond the Headlines special, featuring Tune Talk executive director and chief executive officer Gurtaj Singh Padda, who shared the company's vision behind its newly launched brand direction, "Kasi Tune". According to Gurtaj, Tune Talk's latest positioning reflects a broader ambition that extends beyond connectivity alone.

items.title: Tun Mahathir shares advice at 101: Eat to live, not live to eat [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Former prime minister Tun Dr Mahathir Mohamad shared advice on health and longevity after celebrating his 101st birthday. Eat to live, not live to eat," he said in a brief video posted on his official Facebook page. On July 10, Dr Mahathir thanked Malaysians for the overwhelming outpouring of birthday wishes and greetings.

items.title: S.Korea court sentences ex-president to 2 years in jail in political funding case
items.summary: SEOUL: A South Korean court sentenced former President Yoon Suk Yeol to two years in prison on Monday after finding him guilty of illegally receiving opinion polling services worth 270 million won (US$179,800) for free from a political broker, local media said. The Seoul Central District Court found Yoon violated political funding laws by receiving 14 rounds of polling from the political broker at no cost and later exercising influence over the nomination of a former lawmaker to repay him, according to the ruling. Yoon, 65, is involved in eight legal cases.

items.title: KL Immigration rings up RM10m in compounds, overstayers top list
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 13 — The Kuala Lumpur Immigration Department recorded 845 charges involving 801 foreign nationals for various immigration offences between January and June 30 this year. Its director, Hamsha Injau, said offences under Section 15(4) of the Immigration Act 1959/63 for overstaying accounted for the highest number, with 556 charges. He said the department had also collected RM10.04 million in compounds for various offences under the Immigration Act as of June 30.

items.title: Zahid insists Umno has no PAS pact for Negeri Sembilan, warns ‘goalposts can change anytime’
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 13 — Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi today stressed that the party has no formal agreement with PAS over the upcoming Negeri Sembilan state election, apart from a general understanding between the two sides. According to Berita Harian (BH), Zahid, who is also Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman, confirmed that Umno had never finalised discussions with PAS, including on seat allocations for the polls. “We have no agreement with PAS.

items.title: Federal Court upholds finding of possible pressure on witness in Syed Saddiq trial
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Federal Court today upheld the Court of Appeal's finding that there was evidence that a key prosecution witness in Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman's trial may have been subjected to improper pressure during the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission's (MACC) investigation. Justice Datuk Che Mohd Ruzima Ghazali, delivering the majority judgment, rejected the prosecution's contention that the Court of Appeal had made "wild accusations" against the prosecution and the MACC when it questioned the credibility of prosecution witness Rafiq Hakim Razali. Instead, Ruzima said the appellate court's findings were firmly grounded in evidence adduced during the trial.

items.title: Student held over alleged stalking, harassment of university mate
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: Police have arrested a public university student for allegedly harassing and stalking a female university mate since April. District police chief Assistant Commissioner Sarudin Samah said the suspect was arrested in Seksyen 7 yesterday, following a report lodged by the 22-year-old victim at 3.55pm. Shah Alam police chief Assistant Commissioner Sarudin Samah said the suspect, a man in his 20s, was arrested at about 8.30pm yesterday in Section 7, hours after the victim lodged a police report at 3.55pm.

items.title: MCMC overhaul on the cards as govt tables amendments to 1998 Act
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 13 — The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (Amendment) Bill 2026 was tabled for first reading in the Dewan Rakyat today, proposing to expand the commission’s functions, revise its membership structure and raise the contract value limit it can approve without ministerial approval to RM50 million. Communications Minister Datuk Seri Fahmi Fadzil said the Bill would be tabled for second reading during the current parliamentary sitting. According to the Bill uploaded on Parliament’s official portal, one of the key proposals is an amendment to Section 45 of the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission Act 1998 (Act 589) through Clause 15, which would increase the contract value limit that the commission may enter into on its own authority.

items.title: Why the Federal Court upheld Syed Saddiq's acquittal
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Federal Court upheld Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman's acquittal after ruling that the prosecution failed to prove the essential ingredients of the offences and did not establish any appealable error in the Court of Appeal's findings. In the majority judgment, Justices Datuk Che Mohd Ruzima Ghazali and Datuk Collin Lawrence Sequerah found that the High Court's convictions on all four charges were unsafe, dismissing the prosecution's final appeal. On the charge of abetting criminal breach of trust (CBT) involving RM1 million in Bersatu Youth funds, the majority agreed that the prosecution failed to prove the principal offence allegedly committed by the wing's assistant treasurer Rafiq Hakim Razali.

items.title: Worker deaths come at a price: Occupational safety group calls for workplace inspections to return after Klang factory fire
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 13 — The Malaysian Society for Occupational Safety and Health (MSOSH) has urged the Ministry of Human Resources (KESUMA) to review the need for periodic workplace inspections following the death of a foreign worker in a boiler fire at an iron processing plant in Klang last Friday. MSOSH president Datuk Wira Ahmad Fakhrul Anuar Ismail said regular inspections serve as an important check-and-balance mechanism, allowing employers to identify and address safety shortcomings before accidents happen. Ahmad Fakhrul also extended condolences to the victim’s family, saying the tragedy was a stark reminder that neglecting workplace safety can cost lives and threaten a company’s operations and long-term survival.

items.title: Zahid downplays Nga’s resignation vow, says Unity Government strong and ‘he’s my friend’
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 13 — Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi today played down remarks by Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming, who had pledged to resign if Barisan Nasional (BN) scored a major victory in Johor and former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak were freed. Zahid, who is also BN chairman, said the statement was inappropriate but not worth escalating. On ties within the federal administration following BN’s strong showing in Johor, Zahid stressed that relations in the Unity Government remained solid, HM reported.

items.title: Unicef, NUTP, academics and NGOs all in: Education Ministry goes all-hands-on-deck to tackle student safety crisis
items.summary: IPOH, July 13 — The Ministry of Education (MOE) is strengthening efforts to safeguard students’ mental well-being and enhance school safety through a series of preventive and support initiatives. Deputy Education Minister Wong Kah Woh said the measures include the establishment of the Special Committee on Educational Institution Safety Reform, comprising stakeholders from within the ministry, academic experts, government agencies and non-governmental organisations (NGOs). He said the ministry treated every incident involving students with the utmost seriousness and had instructed all State Education Departments (JPN) and District Education Offices (PPD) to strictly follow existing guidelines and report cases immediately.

items.title: ‘I could have bowed’: Syed Saddiq says six-year ordeal proves principle outweighs fleeting power
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, July 13 — Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman walked out of the Federal Court today with his acquittal intact — declaring the fight was about principle, not just personal freedom. “Malaysia must be led by those willing to lose position for principle, not sell principle for position. I could have stayed silent.

items.title: ‘Cheerful, sociable, active, loving’: In inquest, psychologist says Zara Qairina’s profile challenges suicide theory
items.summary: Child Psychologist Dr Noor Aishah Rosli, 51, who is also a registered counsellor, said the finding was based on an overall assessment of Zara Qairina’s development from birth until she was 13. The 76th witness said the psychological autopsy approach was used to prepare Zara Qairina’s profile, which involved a retrospective forensic psychology assessment of her mental, emotional, social, behavioural, interpersonal and environmental state before her death. “I also evaluated whether there were psychological, social, family, school environment, peer relationship, hostel experience, personal writings, phone communications and witness information factors that could help the Court understand the full context of Zara’s life before the incident,” she said.

items.title: Deputy finance minister pitches one-stop tech financing hub in Penang-Kulim area to power Malaysia’s true ‘Silicon Valley’ dream
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 13 — Deputy Finance Minister Liew Chin Tong has proposed that Malaysia establish a one-stop centre for semiconductor and deep-tech financing, offering integrated financing solutions in the heart of the country’s technology cluster. He said the move would help turn the technology cluster into Malaysia’s true Silicon Valley by bridging the gap between deep tech and capital. Liew said the one-stop centre could support themes, and not only single names, inviting investors to back a well-governed national story built on trust in Malaysia’s regulatory framework.

items.title: Selangor Sultan rides the LRT3 train, says Shah Alam Line should have been completed earlier, but delay had its ‘wisdom’
items.summary: KLANG, July 13 — The Sultan of Selangor today said the Shah Alam LRT3 line should have been completed according to its original schedule, although His Royal Highness added that he believed there was “wisdom” behind the delay. The remarks came in a statement from the Selangor Royal Office issued after Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah travelled on the newly opened line from Bandar Utama station in Petaling Jaya to Johan Setia station in Klang. “It would have been preferable if the LRT3 line had been completed according to its original schedule, as I view the rail service as an essential public transport facility for the convenience of the people.” Sultan Sharafuddin said he was nevertheless grateful the long-awaited rail line had finally become a reality, describing it as a project that would improve the quality of life, ease traffic congestion and provide a more efficient, comfortable and sustainable public transport system.
